overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains a strong, highly specialized professional presence across academic and developer-heavy platforms, focusing on computational social science, disinformation, and social media analysis. With a combined following of 3,580, his posts consistently generate strong engagement, particularly when announcing tangible research outcomes, new tools, and academic opportunities. LinkedIn drives the highest absolute engagement volume, while Bluesky yields the highest relative engagement rate, indicating a highly active and receptive audience on decentralized platforms.

contentThemes.description: Research updates, papers, and presentations on identifying coordinated link sharing and information operations.
Job openings for postdoctoral researchers, PhD opportunities, and major research grant announcements.
Software releases and algorithmic updates for tools like CooRnet used in social media analysis.

recommendations: Capitalize on Bluesky's high engagement rate by using it as the primary platform for immediate research discussions and networking.
Utilize LinkedIn specifically for recruitment and institutional milestones, as it attracts professional comments and has the largest absolute reach.
Integrate visual aids, such as screenshots of the CooRnet tool or graphical abstracts of papers, to further boost engagement across all channels.

platformInsights.bluesky: Acts as a highly vibrant hub for peer interaction, showing the highest engagement rate (average 6.14%), specifically around publication sharing and student opportunities.
platformInsights.mastodon: Attracts a technically inclined, open-source-friendly academic audience, responding exceptionally well to direct tool and software updates like CooRnet v2.0.
platformInsights.linkedin: Functions as the premier professional network, generating the highest overall interactions (likes and comments) for structural career updates, hiring, and grant wins.

topPerformingContent.content: Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
New research opportunity for PhD students interested in computational social science. Reply for details!
Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.
